mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls drives aggregate wage income higher, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% embeds a tightening risk premium that weighs on equity valuations; each month at this level without easing increases the probability of an abrupt repricing at liftoff, generating negative S&P 500 drift over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal Reserve maintained target range of 0 to 0.25% through December 2014
failure_condition: Months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts creates a reinforcing demand impulse that pushes aggregate demand above potential output, driving operating leverage and profit margin expansion for S&P 500 firms over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of month-over-month increases in both RSAFS and HOUST produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the predictive power is driven exclusively by one proxy.

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of recovery from an industrial slowdown, month-over-month rebounds in industrial production signal inventory restocking and capacity utilization gains that outpace cost pressures, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During early-cycle recovery periods, month-over-month increases in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above its 4-quarter trend signals persistent aggregate demand momentum that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 surprises relative to a 4-quarter moving average are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.2 percentage point decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate monetary tightening, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls more than 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points in 3 months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% and industrial production accelerates month-over-month, the real corporate cost of capital falls while capacity utilization rises, creating a profit margin expansion channel that generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 1% and month-over-month INDPRO increase fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or either proxy alone explains the predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below one million units signals a contraction in residential investment that drags construction employment and durable goods consumption lower, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts reported at 1009 thousand units in October 2014
failure_condition: Months where HOUST is below 1000 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
